summary: Closed, unmerged effects-system decomp/naming pass. The PR renamed and reorganized several ef units, especially replacing efspecial.c with efalt.c and eflib_alloc.c with efdata.c in splits/config, added descriptive names for many efLib and efAsync symbols, moved effect allocation/data declarations, and improved partial matching for efSync_Spawn and efAsync_Dispatch. Bot metrics showed new matches for efAlt_Spawn, efdata bss/sbss, efasync sdata, and efAsync_Spawn, but equivalent broken matches from renamed units plus a large efasync .data regression. The author explicitly framed remaining work around jump-table matching versus hardcoding and noted that the current file split may not reflect the original source layout.
what_changed: Renamed the matched special-effect unit in config from melee/ef/efspecial.c to melee/ef/efalt.c; efspecial.c and efspecial.h were removed while efalt.c and efalt.h were added.
Replaced the old eflib_alloc split entry with efdata.c and adjusted bss/sbss ownership: efdata.c gained the 0x80458EB0 bss region and 0x804D64E8-0x804D64F4 sbss region.
Updated configure.py so the ef library builds efdata.c and efalt.c instead of eflib_alloc.c and efspecial.c.

Renamed many efLib symbols from address-style names to descriptive names such as efLib_Init, efLib_SetFlags, efLib_Destroy, efLib_Update, efLib_Create, efLib_Create_Attach, efLib_CreateGenerator, efLib_SpawnParticleEffect, and multiple efLib_Cb_* callback names.

Renamed async symbols from address placeholders to behavior-oriented names including efAsync_Dispatch, efAsync_LoadAsync, efAsync_OnLoad, efAsync_LoadSync, efAsync_QueueProcessDeferred, efAsync_QueueFlush, efAsync_QueueClear, and efAsync_QueueInit.
Renamed efSpecial_SpawnSpecial to efAlt_Spawn and associated local data/sdata/sdata2 labels to efAlt_* names.
Added or revised effect headers and types in eflib.h, efasync.h, efdata.h, forward.h, and types.h to support the new naming/data organization.
Added float and long double math constants in src/MSL/math.h, including M_PI_F, M_PI_2_F, M_PI_3_F, M_PI_L, M_TAU_L, M_PI_2_L, and M_PI_3_L.
Touched call sites in fighter, item, game-mode, stage/vi, grlib, lbdvd, and baselib particle/gobj code, apparently to follow the renamed effect APIs and related type changes.
Introduced temporary particle linkNo skip/render mask definitions in particle-related code according to the PR notes, with TODOs to source them from particle headers once those are fleshed out.
smart_moves: Kept the efalt name temporarily to reduce merge-conflict headache, while documenting that it had previously been named special and could be renamed back after integration.
Used descriptive symbol names to replace address-based efLib and efAsync placeholders, making later call-site reasoning and effect decomp work more searchable.

Recorded the exact dispatch condition for efalt usage: the PR body says it is only called if efSync_Spawn receives a gfx_id in the range 0x478 >= gfx_id < 0x4BA; the inequality wording is odd but should be preserved as author evidence.
Separated matching pragmatism from source-layout truth: the author noted strings suggest there may only have been eflib.c and efasync.c originally, but kept separate files for matching percentages.
Called out unresolved jump-table decisions explicitly instead of silently hardcoding: efAsync_Dispatch and efSync_Spawn were both near 98% while their associated jump tables remained much lower.
Moved data ownership in splits/symbols alongside code renames, which is essential for decomp reports to attribute bss/sbss/data matches to the intended unit.
decomp_lessons: Large function renames should be paired with symbols.txt updates so the semantic names become searchable project-wide and bot reports stay interpretable.

When renaming or splitting units, check bot reports for both new matches and broken matches; here efalt and efdata appeared as new matches while efspecial, eflib_alloc, and eflib sbss showed corresponding broken matches due to ownership changes.

For near-matching jump-table-heavy functions, document whether the blocker is control-flow structure, .data placement, or an artificial hardcoded jump table; this PR leaves efAsync_Dispatch and efSync_Spawn as explicit future targets.
Temporary file organization can be acceptable for matching, but leave notes when strings or evidence suggest the original source layout differed.
Effect-system work spans many consumers; API/type/header renames must be propagated through fighter, item, game-mode, stage/vi, grlib, lbdvd, and baselib particle call sites.
Particle bitmask constants should not remain duplicated indefinitely; this PR notes PTCL_SKIP_LINKNO_* and PTCL_RENDER_LINKNO_* should eventually come from a fleshed-out particle header.
naming_conventions: Effect library public-ish functions use efLib_ prefix with PascalCase action names: efLib_Init, efLib_SetFlags, efLib_Destroy, efLib_Create, efLib_CreateGenerator.

Effect library callback helpers use efLib_Cb_* names, often encoding the transformed field or source parameter: efLib_Cb_SetOffsetY_FromParamY, efLib_Cb_SetScale_FromParamX, efLib_Cb_SetRotY_FromFighterDir.
Generator creation variants are named by composing operations after efLib_CreateGenerator, e.g. _Attach, _Attach_AddAppSRT, _AppSRT_SetScale, _AppSRT_SetFacingDirScale, _AppSRT_SetPos.
Async queue and loading functions use efAsync_ plus verb/object names: LoadAsync, LoadSync, OnLoad, QueueProcessDeferred, QueueFlush, QueueClear, QueueInit.
The PR has some inconsistent casing evidence, such as efLib_remove_user_data and efLib_render_callback alongside PascalCase efLib names; future cleanup should normalize only with matching evidence.
Local data labels were moved from compiler-generated @ labels to unit-scoped names like efAlt_803BF9D0 and jumptable_803BF9F8.
Global data names were made semantic where possible: efAsync_DatEntries, efLib_AnimQueue, efLib_ParamTable, efLib_LoadKind, efLib_EffectCount, efLib_AnimCount.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: efAsync_Dispatch was documented as the only remaining unmatched async function, around 98% matching, with one jump table around 53% and a second one-word jump table around 88%.
efSync_Spawn was documented around 98% matching, with its associated jump table around 60%.
The author was unsure whether to hardcode jump tables or match them by restructuring C/control flow and possibly treating a jump table as a .data-located variable.
Bot report measured efSync_Spawn improving from 95.90% to 98.28% and efAsync_Dispatch improving from 98.46% to 98.60%.

Bot report showed efAsync_Spawn reached 100% and efasync .sdata reached 100%, while efasync .data regressed from 65.93% to 24.82%; future work should inspect data-table naming/layout changes before reusing the approach wholesale.
Splits were edited at exact section boundaries for efdata, eflib, efsync, efalt, and efasync, including small sdata/sbss size corrections.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

The only issue comment was an automated decomp-dev bot report: matched code increased by 656 bytes (+0.02%), matched data increased by 27 bytes, but six matches were reported broken due mainly to file/unit renames and efasync .data regressed.
The author invited renaming feedback, stating they were not attached to the descriptive names used in the PR.
The PR was closed without merge; the slice does not provide a human explanation for the closure.
follow_up_queries: Find the later PR that either merged this ef work or renamed efalt back to efspecial/special.
Search for later changes to efAsync_Dispatch and efSync_Spawn jump table matching, especially around jumptable_803BFB28 and efSync jump table data.
Check whether efasync .data regression from this closed PR was fixed in a subsequent effect PR.
Look for later particle.h work that centralizes PTCL_SKIP_LINKNO_* and PTCL_RENDER_LINKNO_* constants.
Search for naming normalization of efLib_remove_user_data and efLib_render_callback versus PascalCase efLib naming.
